audience: Hackathon viewers, non-technical users, and makers learning how AI products work

workflows.label: Classic concept explanation
workflows.description: Shows how a single concept explanation request moves through the legacy ExplainIt flow.

stages.label: User submits concept
stages.whatHappens: The browser captures the concept and starts a traceable explanation run.
stages.output: Local trace start and request intent

stages.label: Streaming request opens
stages.whatHappens: The client opens an SSE request to the explanation endpoint.

stages.label: Server accepts request
stages.whatHappens: The server validates the request and prepares staged generation.

stages.label: First explanation starts
stages.whatHappens: The model starts generating the first readable explanation.

stages.label: Learning layer starts
stages.whatHappens: The model generates story, steps, and challenge data.

stages.label: Visual layer starts
stages.whatHappens: The model generates structured visual breakdown data.

stages.label: Result merged and saved
stages.whatHappens: The client merges staged outputs, stores local history, and enables sharing.
stages.risk: Partial save or degraded visual layer

events.name: classic-explain.complete
events.safeSummary: The final result was merged and saved locally.

workflows.label: Concept studio pack
workflows.description: Shows how a concept is turned into a structured learning and expression package.

stages.label: User starts studio run
stages.whatHappens: The browser captures the concept, audience, and output intent.
stages.input: Concept label, audience label, and safe mode options

stages.label: Positioning layer starts
stages.whatHappens: The model extracts the concept's essence, value, and target user.
stages.risk: Too generic or mismatched audience framing

stages.label: Mental model layer starts
stages.whatHappens: The model creates a structured mechanism, analogy, and step breakdown.

stages.label: Expression layer starts
stages.whatHappens: The model creates scripts and shareable explanations for different contexts.
stages.output: Script, customer explanation, and community post
stages.risk: Tone mismatch or weak practical example

stages.label: Studio pack completes
stages.whatHappens: The UI merges the studio pack, stores it, and makes each output reusable.

events.name: concept-studio.complete
events.safeSummary: The studio pack was merged and saved locally.

redactionRules: Do not show API keys or provider credentials.
Do not show raw model prompts or complete model responses.
Do not show full request bodies.
Show only workflow ids, run ids, concept labels, stage names, counts, durations, and safe summaries.
